description: The Insurance Boss is a national insurance marketing and consulting platform designed to help individuals and businesses understand, optimize, and protect their insurance coverage. Powered by affiliate licensed agents, they focus on smarter coverage, better pricing, and long-term risk strategy.

general_liability.title: General Liability Insurance
general_liability.description: Protects against customer injuries, property damage, and advertising claims. Includes lawsuit defense coverage.

bop.title: Business Owner's Policy (BOP)
bop.description: A '3-in-1' bundle combining General Liability, Property Insurance, and Business Interruption coverage. Offers average savings of 9%.

workers_comp.title: Workers’ Compensation
workers_comp.description: Mandatory protection covering medical costs and lost wages for work-related injuries; protects businesses from lawsuits.

commercial_auto.title: Commercial Auto Insurance
commercial_auto.description: Coverage for business vehicles with higher limits than personal auto; covers employees while driving for work.

cyber_liability.title: Cyber Liability Insurance
cyber_liability.description: Protection against data breaches, ransomware, and cyber extortion. Includes breach response and business interruption.

employee_benefits.title: Employee Benefits
employee_benefits.description: Secure their future, strengthen your team. Group Health, Group Life, Short/Long-Term Disability, and Retirement Plans (401k).

whole_life.title: Whole Life Insurance
whole_life.description: Lifetime coverage with guaranteed premiums and tax-deferred cash value growth.

universal_life.title: Universal Life Insurance
universal_life.description: Flexible premiums and death benefits with cash value accumulation.

term_life.title: Term Life Insurance
term_life.description: Affordable protection for a set period with tax-free death benefits.

mortgage_protection.title: Mortgage Protection
mortgage_protection.description: Specifically designed to pay off your mortgage if you pass away.

disability.title: Disability Insurance
disability.description: Replaces 50-70% of income if you are unable to work due to illness or injury.

auto.title: Auto Insurance
auto.description: Liability, collision, and comprehensive coverage. Discounts available for multi-car, bundling, and good drivers.

homeowners.title: Homeowners Insurance
homeowners.description: Protects the home structure and personal liability. (Note: Standard policies exclude floods/earthquakes).

umbrella.title: Umbrella Insurance
umbrella.description: Extra liability protection beyond standard limits for those with significant assets.

landlord_dwelling.title: Landlord / Dwelling
landlord_dwelling.description: Specialized coverage for rental properties (structure, liability, and loss of rental income).

flood.title: Flood Insurance
flood.description: Building and contents coverage for external water damage (NFIP or private).

specialty_vehicles.title: Specialty Vehicles
specialty_vehicles.description: Coverage for motorcycles, RVs, boats, ATVs, watercraft, and classic cars.

iras.title: IRAs
iras.description: Traditional and Roth options for tax-advantaged retirement savings.

college_savings.title: College Savings / 529 Plans
college_savings.description: Tax-free growth and withdrawals for qualified education expenses.

annuities.title: Annuities
annuities.description: Contracts providing guaranteed lifetime income and growth.

401k_rollovers.title: 401(k) Rollovers
401k_rollovers.description: Tax-free transfers from former employers to maintain investment control.

faq.question: Is The Insurance Boss an insurance company?
faq.answer: No. The Insurance Boss is a marketing and consulting engine that analyzes coverage and connects clients with licensed insurance professionals and agencies.

faq.question: How does the free policy review work?
faq.answer: Simply upload your current policy. Their AI-powered review tool and licensed professionals analyze it for gaps, overpricing, and missed opportunities at no cost.

faq.question: Am I required to switch insurance after a review?
faq.answer: No. The role is to educate and advise. You are never obligated to switch policies.
